item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Once again, tens of thousands flocked to Churchill Downs, dressed to the nines, to partake in one of the grandest spectacles in sport and, perhaps, travel back in time.

media:content.media:text: Overall view of race track.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A huge crowd at Churchill Downs for the Kentucky Derby.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Race fans pass by the Pat Day statue.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Fans look over the program.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Horses break from the starting gate.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A composite image of people at the Kentucky Derby.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Three men cheer while standing in the infield of Churchill Downs under gray skies.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Horses take off from the starting gate.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A man smokes a cigar while watching the fourth race.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: People wear Derby hats.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Attendees check racing forms of the prep races.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: 152nd Kentucky Derby
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: A jockey holds up a trophy while smiling broadly, surrounded by other celebrants.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Red roses appear in the foreground before the twin spires of Churchill Downs.
media:content.media:description: Jockey Jose Ortiz riding Golden Tempo (#19) toward a first-place finish during the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

item.title: Photos from the 152nd Kentucky Derby: The best day in horse racing, the 'sport of kings'

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Get the latest ahed of the 2026 Kentucky Derby, including how to watch, what time it starts, where to watch or stream the race, who is expected to win and more.

media:content.media:text: A blurred view of a horse and jockey racing down the track.
media:content.media:description: The Puma trains on the track during morning workouts ahead of the running of the 152nd Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on April 29, 2026.

item.title: 2026 Kentucky Derby guide: Time, TV channels, betting favorites and more

item.dc:creator: Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from the Iranian regime to end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed."I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years," the president wrote in a Truth Social post.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Departs White House For Florida
media:content.media:description: Trump's comments come amid increased pressure from Capitol Hill to seek approval for the conflict.

item.title: Trump says he is reviewing Iran's new offer but doubts it is 'acceptable'

item.description: The new images come as the director of the Secret Service says he would not change anything about the security setup the night of the shooting. NBC News’ Kelly O’Donnell reports.

media:content.media:title: Alleged White House dinner gunman had respirator, hollow-point bullets
media:content.media:description: The new images come as the director of the Secret Service says he would not change anything about the security setup the night of the shooting. NBC News’ Kelly O’Donnell reports.

item.title: Alleged White House dinner gunman had respirator, hollow-point bullets

item.description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

media:content.media:title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations
media:content.media:description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

item.title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
item.description: An artificial intelligence model from the Mayo Clinic detected early signs of possible pancreatic cancer on scans up to two years before patients were diagnosed.

media:content.media:text: The REDMOD AI model analyzes a CT scan to detect early tissue changes associated with pancreatic cancer.
media:content.media:description: The Mayo Clinic's AI model analyzes a CT scan to detect early tissue changes associated with pancreatic cancer.

item.title: AI finds signs of pancreatic cancer before tumors develop

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Danco Laboratories, one of makers of the mifepristone abortion pill on Saturday asked the Supreme Court to pause a lower court ruling that imposed a nationwide requirement for the medication to be dispensed in-person.

media:content.media:text: A hand with black fingernails holding up a box of Mifepristone abortion pills in front of the U.S. Supreme Court building.
media:content.media:description: In its filing, Danco Laboratories argued that the lower court went too far by immediately limiting the sale of mifepristone to in-person distribution

item.title: Supreme Court asked to pause ruling blocking telehealth and mail access to abortion pills

item.description: Trump says there is possibility U.S. could strike Iran again

media:content.media:title: Trump says there is possibility U.S. could strike Iran again
media:content.media:description: Trump says there is possibility U.S. could strike Iran again

item.title: Trump says there is possibility U.S. could strike Iran again

item.description: Pickup truck drives off a cliff in South Africa

media:content.media:title: Pickup truck drives off a cliff in South Africa
media:content.media:description: Pickup truck drives off a cliff in South Africa

item.title: Pickup truck drives off a cliff in South Africa

item.description: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war

media:content.media:title: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war
media:content.media:description: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war

item.title: Man climbs D.C. bridge to protest Iran war

item.description: Last Spirit flights seen descending into Dallas airport

media:content.media:title: Last Spirit flights seen descending into Dallas airport
media:content.media:description: Last Spirit flights seen descending into Dallas airport

item.title: Last Spirit flights seen descending into Dallas airport

item.description: Man says Waymo drove away with his luggage

media:content.media:title: Man says Waymo drove away with his luggage
media:content.media:description: Man says Waymo drove away with his luggage

item.title: Man says Waymo drove away with his luggage

item.description: Transportation Sec. Sean Duffy tells reporters that the collapse of Spirit Arlines was years in the making, blaming the Biden administration’s denial of a merger with JetBlue in 2024, and not the recent Iran war, which has sent jet fuel costs soaring.

media:content.media:title: Trump admin says Iran war not to blame for Spirit shutdown
media:content.media:description: Transportation Sec. Sean Duffy tells reporters that the collapse of Spirit Arlines was years in the making, blaming the Biden administration’s denial of a merger with JetBlue in 2024, and not the recent Iran war, which has sent jet fuel costs soaring.

item.title: Trump admin says Iran war not to blame for Spirit shutdown

item.description: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit

media:content.media:title: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit
media:content.media:description: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit

item.title: Duffy explains why there was no bailout of Spirit

item.description: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport

media:content.media:title: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport
media:content.media:description: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport

item.title: Passengers learn of Spirit's shutdown at the airport

item.description: Flash flooding kills at least one person in Texas

media:content.media:description: Flash flooding kills at least one person in Texas

item.title: Flash flooding kills at least one person in Texas

item.description: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany

media:content.media:description: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany

item.title: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany

item.description: Federal court blocks access to abortion pills by mail

media:content.media:title: Federal court blocks access to abortion pills by mail
media:content.media:description: Federal court blocks access to abortion pills by mail

item.title: Federal court blocks access to abortion pills by mail

item.description: NBC News' Ryan Nobles joins "Here's the Scoop" to report on contradictory statements from Republicans on the state of the Iran war and how that messaging could affect midterm elections.

media:content.media:title: How Republican messaging on Iran war could affect midterms
media:content.media:description: NBC News' Ryan Nobles joins "Here's the Scoop" to report on contradictory statements from Republicans on the state of the Iran war and how that messaging could affect midterm elections.

item.title: How Republican messaging on Iran war could affect midterms

item.description: Spirit Airlines could shut down without bailout

media:content.media:title: Spirit Airlines could shut down without bailout
media:content.media:description: Spirit Airlines could shut down without bailout

item.title: Spirit Airlines could shut down without bailout

item.dc:creator: Dareh Gregorian
Megan Shannon
item.description: The Trump administration has made an aggressive push to add the president's name to buildings, battleships, money and government websites.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: DONALD J. TRUMP etched into a wet stone facade, with snow covering the grass beneath it.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Workers in two hydraulic cranes are suspended above the ground in front of the Kennedy Center's facade, with a giant purple tarp stretched behind them.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump stands next to a signboard with an image of a battleship inside a room at Mar-a-Lago.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Designs for Semiquincentennial gold coins featuring President Trump.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: A 50 dollar bill poking up from a seat pocket in President Trump's pants as he ascends the stairs to Air Force One.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: The State Department will be releasing a limited series of U.S. passports featuring the image of President Trump, a State Department official said
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: Image: Workers install a new banner featuring an image of President Donald Trump on the facade of the U.S. Department of Justice
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump speaks at at a "Trump Accounts" event on Jan. 28, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Announces TrumpRx From The White House
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is physically leaving his mark on Washington and beyond, more so than any other president in modern U.S. history.

item.title: 12 places Trump's name or image is being added by the federal government

item.dc:creator: Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from the Iranian regime to end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed."I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years," the president wrote in a Truth Social post.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Departs White House For Florida
media:content.media:description: Trump's comments come amid increased pressure from Capitol Hill to seek approval for the conflict.

item.title: Trump says he is reviewing Iran's new offer but doubts it is 'acceptable'

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Dennis Romero
item.description: Defense lawyers for the man charged with trying to assassinate President Donald Trump during the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner last week say he’s being deprived of his dignity and resources by being unnecessarily kept under suicide precaution status.

media:content.media:text: Red and blue lights atop law enforcement vehicles illuminate a city streetscape at night.
media:content.media:description: Police surround the Washington Hilton Hotel where shots were fired near the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington on April 25, 2026.

item.title: White House Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect on suicide precautions, lawyers say

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: Maine Democratic Party convention-goers were relieved to focus on the general election, but some had mixed feelings about their prospects against GOP Sen. Susan Collins.

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner speaks
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, a Maine Democrat and U.S. Senate candidate, during an interview in York on Apr. 17.

item.title: Graham Platner looks to unite Maine Democrats as 'presumptive' Senate nominee

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: Cherie DeVaux became the first woman to train the winning horse at the Kentucky Derby when Golden Tempo claimed the 152nd Run for the Roses on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Cherie DeVaux smiles while holding a trophy surrounded by other people.
media:content.media:description: Trainer Cherie DeVaux celebrating following the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: 152nd Kentucky Derby
media:content.media:description: Trainer Cherie DeVaux celebrating following the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Ky. on Saturday.

item.title: Kentucky Derby winner Cherie DeVaux bet on herself and won big
